message:
chore(beads): update audit tracking — batches 351-354 (26 files, ~25K lines, all SOUND)

SapphireHill audit session: 4 batches completed, 0 bugs found.
- Batch 351: fs/{uring,path_ops,vfs,file}, cli/{progress,completion}, signal/signal
- Batch 352: codec/{framed,framed_read,framed_write,length_delimited,lines}, grpc/protobuf, net/quic/connection
- Batch 353: net/unix/stream, time/interval, trace/{delta_debug,distributed/crdt}, grpc/server, net/websocket/split, lab/replay
- Batch 354: distributed/recovery, service/{buffer,load_balance}, net/quic_native/streams, plan/rewrite

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.36","title":"[audit][runtime] Finish uninterruptible JoinFuture migration in task_handle","description":"Current worktree changes partially switch TaskHandle::join()/join_with_drop_reason() to recv_uninterruptible(), but the shared lib frontier still reports JoinFuture construction type mismatches in src/runtime/task_handle.rs. Finish the migration coherently, keep drop/cancel semantics intact, add focused regression coverage if behavior changes, and validate with rch-backed targeted commands.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"WhiteCliff","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:08:15.451211487Z","created_by":"WhiteCliff","updated_at":"2026-03-09T02:11:08.855625200Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T02:11:08.855600714Z","close_reason":"Already fixed in current tree by concurrent join migration; validated via rch cargo check -p asupersync --lib","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","compile","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.36","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:08:15.451211487Z","created_by":"WhiteCliff","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.37","title":"[audit][service] Mark refresh_pending when Reconnect::call panics","description":"Reconnect::call currently invokes inner.call(req) directly and only the returned ReconnectFuture can set refresh_pending. If inner.call(req) panics synchronously while constructing the future, no ReconnectFuture exists, so refresh_pending stays false and the wrapper can keep reusing the same broken inner service on the next poll_ready. Add an unwind guard that marks refresh_pending on synchronous panic, add a focused regression, and validate with rch-backed check/clippy/test commands.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:11:41.778678308Z","created_by":"PinkForest","updated_at":"2026-03-09T02:13:27.006584161Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T02:13:26.998357304Z","close_reason":"Completed: reconnect now marks refresh_pending on synchronous inner.call panic, with focused regression and targeted rch validation.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","service"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.37","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:11:41.778678308Z","created_by":"PinkForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.38","title":"[audit][rate_limit] Restore consumed rate-limit token if guarded operation or web handler panics","description":"Both the core combinator and the sync web adapter consume a rate-limit token before invoking user code. In src/combinator/rate_limit.rs, RateLimiter::call_weighted() acquires tokens and then runs op() without an unwind guard, so a synchronous panic burns tokens even though the operation never completed. In src/web/middleware.rs, RateLimitMiddleware::call() uses limiter.try_acquire() and then calls the inner handler directly, so a panicking handler leaks the consumed token in the same way. Add a narrow refund path for panic unwind, route the web middleware through the panic-safe helper, add focused regression coverage in both modules, and validate with rch-backed targeted check/clippy/test commands.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"PinkForest","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-03-09T02:29:33.537070930Z","created_by":"PinkForest","updated_at":"2026-03-09T02:50:02.362203392Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T02:50:02.362173717Z","close_reason":"Refund consumed rate-limit tokens on synchronous panic in combinator and web middleware; focused rch regressions passed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","combinator","rate-limit","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.38","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:29:33.537070930Z","created_by":"PinkForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Plan: 1) Audit hot-path allocations in scheduler/cancellation code, 2) Add zero-allocation test guards, 3) Create E2E stress run with allocation ceilings, 4) Add CI guard for allocation regression.","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:31:02Z"},{"id":1145,"issue_id":"asupersync-20c57","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DONE: tests/allocation_audit.rs - 12 tests, all pass with default parallelism, zero clippy warnings","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:50:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-20caz","title":"HTTP/1 client bug: Http1Client::request never flushes encoded request","description":"## Symptom

HTTP/1 requests that go through `Http1Client::request` hang until timeout because the encoded request bytes are never flushed to the socket.

This breaks downstream consumers (notably `mcp_agent_mail_rust` JWKS fetch), where the in-process JWKS server never sees the GET request and the client hits its timeout.

## Root Cause

File: `src/http/h1/client.rs`

`Http1Client::request` does:
- `framed.send(req)?;` (buffers into write buffer)
- immediately starts polling for response frames

But it never calls `poll_flush`/flush on the framed writer after `send`, so nothing is written to the underlying IO.

The `request_streaming` code path works because it calls `io.write_all(...)` directly.

## Fix

After `framed.send(req)` in `Http1Client::request`, flush the framed writer (e.g. `futures::future::poll_fn(|cx| framed.poll_flush(cx)).await`). Map any `io::Error` into the existing `HttpError` type.

## Tests

Add a regression test that:
- starts a local TCP listener
- reads until it sees the request line/headers
- writes a minimal HTTP/1.1 response
- asserts the client receives it

Test must fail on current main and pass after the flush fix.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-06T02:47:27.642239083Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T02:55:43.492935249Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T02:55:43.492914450Z","close_reason":"Fix Http1Client::request to flush after send; add regression test; unblocks downstream JWKS fetch","external_ref":"bd-2vgzb","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

All pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","runtime","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-20cm1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-4sdk5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:50Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1357,"issue_id":"asupersync-20cm1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Every test function must: (1) call init_test_logging() at start; (2) use test_phase!("test_name") for the top-level test marker; (3) use test_section!("phase") for each distinct phase (setup, execution, verification, teardown); (4) use assert_with_log!() instead of bare assert!() for all assertions that compare values; (5) call test_complete!("test_name") on success; (6) log key intermediate state (e.g., connection count, region tree depth, bytes transferred) at INFO level with tracing::info!() so failures can be diagnosed from log output alone.","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:29:46Z"}]}
+{"id":"asupersync-20cz0","title":"Fresh-eyes audit batch 357: bytes/, util/, fs/ modules","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T08:18:26.979543312Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T08:18:26.979543312Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"asupersync-20dwn","title":"[EPIC] Cancel-Aware Synchronization Primitives (tokio-sync equivalent)","description":"# Cancel-Aware Synchronization Primitives

## Overview
Synchronization primitives that integrate with cancellation protocol and support two-phase patterns where needed.

## Components

### 1. Mutex
- lock() returns LockGuard (obligation)
- try_lock() for non-blocking
- Cancel-aware: waiting for lock respects cancellation
- Unlock is automatic via Drop

### 2. RwLock
- read() returns ReadGuard
- write() returns WriteGuard
- Upgradable read locks
- Cancel-aware waiting

### 3. Semaphore
- Two-phase: acquire_permit() returns Permit obligation
- Permit must be released or explicitly dropped
- Bounded and unbounded variants
- OwnedPermit for 'static lifetime

### 4. Barrier
- wait() blocks until N waiters
- Returns BarrierWaitResult (is_leader)
- Cancel-aware: if cancelled, barrier may never complete

### 5. Notify
- notify_one(), notify_waiters()
- notified() returns future
- Cancel-aware waiting

### 6. OnceCell
- get_or_init() with async initializer
- get_or_try_init() for fallible init
- Cancel-aware: racing initializers handled correctly

### 7. Watch Channel
- Single-producer, multi-consumer
- Receivers get latest value
- Cancel-aware subscription

## Invariants
- No deadlocks in well-formed programs
- Cancel-safe acquisition
- Obligations properly tracked
- Deterministic behavior in lab runtime

## Testing
- Concurrent access patterns
- Cancellation during wait
- Deadlock detection (debug builds)
- Deterministic scheduling in lab
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:32:08.464962741Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T16:19:19.114867972Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T16:19:19.114867972Z","close_reason":"All child tasks complete: Mutex, RwLock, Semaphore, Barrier, Notify, OnceCell implemented with tests. Watch Channel deferred to separate task if needed.","external_ref":"bd-q48","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-20dwn","depends_on_id":"asupersync-106cq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:09Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-20fk9","title":"[SPORK][App][Docs] Minimal supervised app example (OTP mental model)","notes":"Implemented and validated a runnable minimal supervised Spork app example.

Delivered:
- Added examples/spork_minimal_supervised_app.rs (compile-checked runnable example).
- Updated docs/integration.md tutorial section with concrete run command and invariant checklist.
- Added README pointer under Spork 'Where To Look'.

Example demonstrates:
- AppSpec start as region-owned supervision tree.
- Supervisor starts named GenServer child (counter service).
- Client cast + call roundtrip with deterministic scheduling.
- Cancel-correct shutdown path via AppHandle::stop + region cancel/drain.
- Name lease resolves on termination (stop_and_release + unregister; whereis none).

Validation run:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo check --all-targets
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
- cargo run --example spork_minimal_supervised_app
- cargo test
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggyMarsh","created_at":"2026-02-06T05:43:31.051028305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:52:41.723342Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:52:41.723277059Z","close_reason":"Completed: added runnable minimal supervised Spork app example + docs wiring + full quality gates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["app","docs","examples","spork"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-20fk9","depends_on_id":"asupersync-17rvx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:40Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-20fk9","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1a4gu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:40Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-20fk9","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2q686","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:40Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-20fk9","depends_on_id":"asupersync-37wzm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:40Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-20fk9","depends_on_id":"asupersync-orj4d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:40Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-20fk9","depends_on_id":"asupersync-tei9b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:40Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1277,"issue_id":"asupersync-20fk9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Goal

Add a **minimal** example that teaches the OTP mental model in Spork terms:

- start an app
- supervisor starts a named GenServer
- client calls/casts
- demonstrate cancel-correct shutdown

## Constraints

- deterministic output; no stdout spam
- emphasize: region close => quiescence
- show name lease resolves on termination

## Acceptance

- Example compiles as a doc test or `examples/` binary (TBD).
- Walking through it is sufficient to understand "Spork = OTP compiled to regions".

 {"id":"asupersync-2is68","title":"[AA][Formal] Proof‑carrying trace certificate spec","description":"## Goal
Specify a proof‑carrying trace certificate: a compact witness that a run respected invariants.

## Why
We want auditable correctness: each trace should include a machine‑verifiable certificate.

## Deliverables
- Certificate schema (hashes, invariants, obligations, cancellation phases).
- Verification algorithm with complexity bounds.
- Mapping from trace events to certificate updates.

## Acceptance
- Certificate verifier rejects invalid traces.
- Certificate is stable under replay; size overhead < target budget.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:05:57.747040779Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:41:25.643591036Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T02:41:25.643506789Z","close_reason":"Added proof-carrying trace certificate spec to formal semantics docs","external_ref":"bd-1ffb","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2is68","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3lwme","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2is68","depends_on_id":"asupersync-m3lue","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"asupersync-2iw9w","title":"[LEAN-COVERAGE][T2] Stabilize tactic behavior and eliminate recursion-depth failures","description":"Resolve failures caused by non-terminating simp/omega/rewrite patterns and unstable tactic behavior.

Scope:

Acceptance Criteria:
- No recursion-depth exhaustion in targeted frontier.
- Tactic runtime and output are stable across repeated runs.","notes":"[2026-02-11 MagentaBridge] Completed tactic-stability/recursion-depth hardening pass in formal/lean/Asupersync.lean.

  - Severity.le_total
  - Severity.le_trans
  - Severity.le_refl
  - Severity.rank_eq_of_le_le
- Reworked severity_monotone_not_restartable proof shape to avoid recursive simplification failure mode.

Acceptance evidence:

     - target/lean-e2e/bd-kf0mv_repeat1_lake_build.log
     - target/lean-e2e/bd-kf0mv_repeat2_lake_build.log

   - Stable totals in both runs: diagnostics=113, errors=58, warnings=55, bucket_count=8.

Artifacts refreshed:
- formal/lean/coverage/lean_frontier_buckets_v1.json regenerated from repeat2 run.

Quality gates:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo check --all-targets
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
- cargo test

 {"id":"asupersync-2ixen","title":"[AA][Formal] Proof harness CI integration","description":"## Goal
Integrate the formal model/proofs into CI so correctness proofs are verified continuously.

## Why
Proofs are only useful if they stay in sync with code. CI prevents drift.

## Deliverables
- CI target that runs proof checks.
- Versioned proof artifacts referenced in docs.
- Failure modes are clear and actionable.

## Acceptance
- CI fails on proof breakage.
- Contributors can reproduce proof checks locally.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:06:07.234929612Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:05:23.259214104Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T03:05:23.259143923Z","close_reason":"Added proof-checks CI job to ci.yml (certificate, obligation, oracle, cancel, laws, DPOR, TLA+, canonicalize tests + artifact upload) and scripts/run_proof_checks.sh for local reproduction","external_ref":"bd-1o2y","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2ixen","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1ky0b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:19Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2ixen","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2o0c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:19Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2ixen","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3lwme","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:19Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
